description: In 2011, Coca-Cola Australia launched the 'Share a Coke' campaign, replacing its logo with popular first names to encourage user-generated sharing. While initially praised for ingenuity, the campaign faced a polarizing debate regarding the normalization of mass-market surveillance and the 'commodification of identity' in social media marketing.

key_events.description: Campaign launch in Australia leads to massive social media photo sharing.
Critics publish op-eds about the shallow commodification of personal identity.
Coca-Cola reports record sales growth, tempering criticism with financial success.
Global rollout begins, cementing the model as a marketing industry standard.

calibration_notes: Useful for assessing the tension between creative user engagement and privacy-centric critiques of corporate brand strategies.
